traits.name: Hold Breath
traits.description: The hydra can hold its breath for 1 hour.

traits.name: Multiple Heads
traits.description: The hydra has five heads. While it has more than one head, the hydra has advantage on saving throws against being blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, stunned, and knocked unconscious. Whenever the hydra takes 25 or more damage in a single turn, one of its heads dies. If all its heads die, the hydra dies. At the end of its turn, it grows two heads for each of its heads that died since its last turn, unless it has taken fire damage since its last turn. The hydra regains 10 hit points for each head regrown in this way.

traits.name: Reactive Heads
traits.description: For each head the hydra has beyond one, it gets an extra reaction that can be used only for opportunity attacks.

traits.name: Wakeful
traits.description: While the hydra sleeps, at least one of its heads is awake.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.description: The hydra makes as many bite attacks as it has heads.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Attack Roll: +8, reach 10 ft. Hit: 10 (1d10 + 5) Piercing damage.

tactical_info.environment: Swamps, marshes, lake shores, underground waterways
tactical_info.tactics: Uses multiple heads for numerous bite attacks, regenerates heads unless damaged by fire, controls large areas with reach and multiple opportunity attacks
tactical_info.weaknesses: Fire damage prevents regeneration, relatively slow movement, predictable attack patterns
signature_abilities: Head regeneration (2 heads per lost head)
